alignments.pub_claim_text: The insights from the analysis of the current data-centric AI landscape support the utility and relevance of the DC-Check framework.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's stronger claim that DC-Check 'addresses the gap' is softened in the publication to support for its utility and relevance based on analysis, indicating a revised (weaker) formulation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DC-Check companion website will serve as an updated resource as methods and tooling in data-centric AI evolve over time.
alignments.rationale: The publication claims list does not mention a companion website or its ongoing updating, so this specific practical commitment appears omitted in the published claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Data-centric AI is still in its infancy and lacks a standardized framework for data-centric considerations in the ML pipeline.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a general statement about the field's infancy and lack of standardized frameworks which is not explicit among the preprint claims, so it is an added contextual/limitation claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed interpretable deep learning model predicts the presence of harmful brainwave patterns and provides high-quality case-based explanations of its decisions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieved AUROCs of 0.87, 0.93, 0.96, 0.92, 0.93, and 0.80 for the classes seizure, LPD, GPD, LRDA, GRDA, and other patterns, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives concrete predictive performance that supports the preprint's claim of predictive ability but does not explicitly restate the "case-based explanations" aspect, so the claim is clarified with quantitative results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The interpretable model performs better than the corresponding black box model while maintaining interpretability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the interpretable model was significantly higher than that of a corresponding uninterpretable black-box model.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparative performance claim and strengthens it by reporting a significant improvement, thus amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned 2D embedded space offers the first global overview of the structure of ictal-interictal-injury continuum brainwave patterns.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results indicate that the ictal-interictal injury continuum hypothesis is supported by data.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's claim of a novel global overview via a 2D embedding is reframed in the publication as empirical support for the continuum hypothesis, which revises and narrows the original framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Understanding the model's decision-making process will help clinicians diagnose and treat harmful brain activities more accurately.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The interpretable design of the deep-learning model facilitates effective human-AI collaboration and may improve diagnosis and patient care in clinical settings.
alignments.rationale: The publication links interpretability to improved human-AI collaboration and possible improvements in diagnosis/care, which clarifies the original claim but uses more cautious language ('may'), tempering confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model could increase trust and adoption of machine learning models in clinical practice among ICU neurologists.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The interpretable design of the deep-learning model facilitates effective human-AI collaboration and may improve diagnosis and patient care in clinical settings.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes facilitation of collaboration and potential clinical benefit rather than explicitly claiming increased trust and adoption, which narrows and tempers the original adoption-focused assertion.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of all users significantly improved when provided with AI assistance, with mean user diagnostic accuracy increasing from 47% to 71%.
alignments.rationale: The user-study result about improved human diagnostic accuracy with AI assistance is new in the publication and was not asserted in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recovering the rank-1 components of a random order-3 symmetric tensor is possible in principle when r is less than or equal to n squared.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The problem of recovering the rank-1 components of an n-dimensional order-3 symmetric tensor is possible in principle when r is less than or equal to n squared.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same theoretical result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Polynomial-time algorithms for tensor decomposition are only known in the regime where r is much less than n to the power of 3/2.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Polynomial-time algorithms for tensor decomposition are only known in the regime where r is much less than n to the power of 3/2.
alignments.rationale: This claim appears unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computational complexity of random order-3 tensor decomposition can be resolved in the low-degree polynomial model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best known algorithms for tensor decomposition cannot be improved by known approaches, as suggested by the results obtained.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the preprint's complexity-resolution claim as evidence that known algorithmic approaches cannot improve existing algorithms; this reframes and slightly clarifies the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: O(log n)-degree polynomial functions of the tensor entries can accurately estimate the largest component when r is much less than n to the power of 3/2 but fail when r is much greater than n to the power of 3/2.
alignments.pub_claim_text: O(logn)-degree polynomial functions of the tensor entries can accurately estimate the largest component when r is much less than n to the power of 3/2 but fail to do so when r is much greater than n to the power of 3/2.
alignments.rationale: This technical statement is restated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A natural extension of the result holds for tensors of any fixed order k greater than or equal to 3, with the LDP threshold being r proportional to n to the power of k/2.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A natural extension of the result holds for tensors of any fixed order k greater than or equal to 3, with the LDP threshold being r proportional to n to the power of k/2.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the extension-to-higher-orders claim with similar wording and status.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study develops and validates models to nowcast observed pollution levels using publicly available Web search data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating web search data with meteorological data improves the nowcasting performance for all three pollutants.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifically states that adding web search data improves nowcasting performance, which clarifies and supports the preprint's development/validation claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel machine learning-based models utilize both traditional supervised classification methods and state-of-the-art deep learning methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not explicitly restate the methodological composition of models (traditional vs deep learning), so this specific methodological claim is absent from the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models predict three critical air pollutants (ozone (O3), nitrogen dioxide (NO2), and fine particulate matter (PM2.5)) across ten major U.S. metropolitan statistical areas (MSAs) in 2017 and 2018.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The top-performing model achieved an accuracy of 0.82 for O<sub>3</sub>, 0.74 for nitrogen dioxide, and 0.85 for PM<sub>2.5</sub> in detecting elevated pollution levels.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides pollutant-level performance metrics that validate the preprint's claim of predicting the three pollutants, though it omits the explicit "ten MSAs in 2017/2018" detail in the summarized claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Prior models based on physical measurement data may not be suitable for predicting public health effects experienced from pollution exposure.
alignments.rationale: The publication's summarized claims focus on predictive performance and applications and do not include this limitation about prior models and public-health suitability, so the claim is not present in the publication claims list.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieved superior accuracy compared to using only meteorological data.
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts a direct performance improvement over meteorological-data-only baselines, which is not explicitly stated in the preprint claims list provided.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study suggests promising novel applications for tracking global physical phenomena using web search data.
alignments.rationale: This broader suggestion about novel applications appears in the publication claims but was not present among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QuerySnout (QS) is the first method to automatically discover vulnerabilities in query-based systems (QBSs).
alignments.pub_claim_text: QuerySnout is the first method to automatically discover vulnerabilities in query-based systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution claim that QuerySnout is the first such automated discovery method.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QS consistently equates or outperforms the best attacks from the literature by a large margin.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The attacks found by QuerySnout consistently equate or outperform the best attacks from the literature.
alignments.rationale: The performance comparison claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QS can be extended to QBSes that require a budget and can be applied to various protection mechanisms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: QuerySnout can be extended to query-based systems that require a budget.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the extensibility claim (budgeted systems); the broader applicability to various protection mechanisms is consistent with this and preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The attacks found by QS allow for highly complex QBSes to be automatically tested 'at the pressing of a button'.
alignments.pub_claim_text: QuerySnout allows for highly complex query-based systems to be automatically tested 'at the pressing of a button'.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the usability/automation claim directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Time series anomaly detection has applications in manufacturing and healthcare.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Examples of deep anomaly detection in time series across various application domains in recent years are provided.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides application-domain examples which aligns with the preprint's statement about applications in manufacturing and healthcare.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The presence of anomalies can indicate novel or unexpected events, such as production faults or heart fluttering.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Examples of deep anomaly detection in time series across various application domains in recent years are provided.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives concrete examples across domains, which clarifies the preprint's illustrative claim about anomalies indicating unexpected events, though it does not repeat the exact examples verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This survey provides a structured and comprehensive overview of state-of-the-art time series anomaly detection models using deep learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The survey provides a structured and comprehensive overview of state-of-the-art deep learning for time series anomaly detection.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the central contribution of the survey as described in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study discusses the advantages and limitations of various anomaly detection techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The advantages and limitations of basic anomaly detection techniques in each category are discussed.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that advantages and limitations are discussed for each category.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research highlights open issues and challenges in adopting deep anomaly detection models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study summarises open issues in research and challenges faced while adopting deep anomaly detection models to time series data.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the preprint's statement about open issues and adoption challenges.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study includes a taxonomy based on anomaly detection strategies and deep learning models.
alignments.rationale: The inclusion of an explicit taxonomy appears in the publication claims but was not listed among the preprint claims provided.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FedLesScan reduces training time and cost by an average of 8% and 20% respectively compared to other approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FedLesScan reduces training time and cost by an average of 8% and 20% respectively compared to other approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same quantitative claim with identical wording and certainty; no semantic, scope, or confidence change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FedLesScan utilizes clients better with an average increase in the effective update ratio of 17.75%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FedLesScan utilizes clients better with an average increase in the effective update ratio of 17.75%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is reproduced verbatim in the publication with the same certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Serverless FL systems can be more resource-efficient and cheaper than conventional FL systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Serverless FL systems can be more resource-efficient and cheaper than conventional FL systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion appears unchanged in wording and expressed certainty in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stragglers in serverless FL systems are caused by slow clients due to their resource and statistical heterogeneity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stragglers in serverless FL systems are caused by slow clients due to their resource and statistical heterogeneity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same causal attribution with identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The existence of functional neuronal ensembles and hubs contributes to the efficiency of information processing. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The existence of functional neuronal ensembles and hubs contributes to the efficiency of information processing. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion verbatim with the same expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The assumption of stationarity in Bayesian inference models impedes inference, leading to destabilization of results and degradation of accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The assumption of stationarity in Bayesian inference models impedes inference, leading to destabilization of results and degradation of accuracy. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The textual claim is the same, though the publication frames it as a 'limitation' rather than a 'result'; the substantive content and certainty are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model can express the neuronal state in a larger space compared to previous studies. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model can express the neuronal state in a larger space compared to previous studies. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same but the publication expresses greater certainty (medium -> high), so confidence is amplified while meaning and scope remain.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generalization of the model without restriction of binary input enables soft clustering and application to non-stationary neuroactivity data. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generalization of the model without restriction of binary input enables soft clustering and application to non-stationary neuroactivity data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording and certainty are the same, though the publication labels it as a 'result' rather than a 'contribution'; the substantive claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recent developments in computer vision and machine learning have facilitated the automatic processing of X-ray images. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent developments in computer vision and machine learning have facilitated the automatic processing of X-ray images. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this high-certainty conclusion verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning has been widely utilized in studies related to X-ray image processing applications. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning has been widely utilized in studies related to X-ray image processing applications. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim is reproduced exactly with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The survey reviews recent research on the application of computer vision and machine learning for X-ray analysis in industrial production and security. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The survey reviews recent research on the application of computer vision and machine learning for X-ray analysis in industrial production and security. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is identical in wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The survey highlights drawbacks in the published research and provides recommendations for future research in computer vision-based X-ray analysis. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The survey highlights drawbacks in the published research and provides recommendations for future research in computer vision-based X-ray analysis. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this limitation and recommendation claim with identical wording and expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural networks are correlation machines that model biases within the data rather than focusing on useful causal relationships. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural networks are correlation machines that model biases within the data rather than focusing on useful causal relationships. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-certainty conclusion without modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI community has concentrated efforts on correcting algorithmic biases to prevent unfair decision-making. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI community has concentrated efforts on correcting algorithmic biases to prevent unfair decision-making. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical wording and certainty are preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This survey paper provides an in-depth overview of the main debiasing methods for fairness-aware neural networks in vision and language research. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This survey paper provides an in-depth overview of the main debiasing methods for fairness-aware neural networks in vision and language research. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is repeated verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel taxonomy is proposed to better organize the literature on debiasing methods for fairness. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel taxonomy tailored for deep learning research is proposed to better organize the literature on debiasing methods for fairness. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the qualifier 'tailored for deep learning research' (clarifying and narrowing the scope) and increases expressed certainty (medium -> high).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper discusses current challenges, trends, and important future work directions for researchers and practitioners in the field. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper discusses current challenges, trends, and important future work directions for researchers and practitioners in the field. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The substantive claim is preserved though the publication frames it as a 'conclusion' rather than a 'contribution'; wording and certainty remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI4Food-NutritionDB database is the first nutrition database that incorporates food images and a nutrition taxonomy based on recommendations by national and international health authorities. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI4Food-NutritionDB database is the first nutrition database that incorporates food images and a nutrition taxonomy based on recommendations by national and international health authorities. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The database offers a multi-level categorisation, comprising 6 nutritional levels, 19 main categories, 73 subcategories, and 893 specific food products. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The database offers a multi-level categorisation, comprising 6 nutritional levels, 19 main categories, 73 subcategories, and 893 specific food products. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical quantitative and structural description is present in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI4Food-NutritionDB opens the doors to new food computing approaches in terms of food intake frequency, quality, and categorisation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI4Food-NutritionDB opens the doors to new food computing approaches in terms of food intake frequency, quality, and categorisation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this conclusion with identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study presents a standardised experimental protocol and benchmark including three tasks based on the nutrition taxonomy. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study presents a standardised experimental protocol and benchmark including three tasks based on the nutrition taxonomy. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is restated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning models trained on AI4Food-NutritionDB achieve accurate recognition results for challenging food image databases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning models trained on AI4Food-NutritionDB achieve accurate recognition results for challenging food image databases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance/result claim with identical wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Static functional connectivity (FC) consistently has inferior prediction performance compared to region level time series or dynamic FC for unimodal rest and task fMRI experiments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Static functional connectivity (FC) shows consistently inferior performance compared to region-level time series or dynamic FC for predicting intelligence. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Texts state the same result about static FC giving inferior prediction performance for intelligence; wording and scope are effectively identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed bi-LSTM pipeline based on region level time series identifies several shared and differential important brain regions across task and rest fMRI experiments that drive intelligence prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed bi-LSTM pipeline identifies several shared and differential important brain regions across fMRI experiments that drive intelligence prediction. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same identification claim but with lower reported certainty; content and scope are the same so meaning is unchanged though confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A test-retest analysis of the selected features shows strong reliability across cross-validation folds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The test-retest analysis of the selected brain regions shows strong reliability across cross-validation folds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the same test-retest reliability result with comparable certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Superior prediction of intelligence can be achieved by accounting for temporal variations in fMRI. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results provide strong evidence that superior prediction of intelligence can be achieved by accounting for temporal variations in fMRI. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both claims make the same conclusion that accounting for temporal variations improves intelligence prediction; publication language slightly emphasizes evidence but does not change meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new method (MR-NOM) was developed for the instance segmentation of cells in Nissl-stained histological images of the brain. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new method (MR-NOM) was developed for the instance segmentation of cells in Nissl-stained histological images of the brain. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Exact same contribution claim in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MR-NOM exploits a multi-scale approach to deliberately over-segment the cells into superpixels and subsequently merge them via a classifier based on shape, structure, and intensity features. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MR-NOM exploits a multi-scale approach to deliberately over-segment the cells into superpixels and subsequently merge them via a classifier based on shape, structure, and intensity features. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Method description is identical between versions; classification label changed but semantic content is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method was tested on images of the cerebral cortex, proving successful in dealing with cells of varying characteristics that partially touch or overlap. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method was tested on images of the cerebral cortex, proving successful in dealing with cells of varying characteristics that partially touch or overlap. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Testing context and success claim are the same in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MR-NOM shows better performance than two state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MR-NOM shows better performance than two state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance comparison claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: State-of-the-art pixel-level segmentation models fail in naively learning anatomical segmentation tasks due to domain memorization issues and conflicting labels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: State-of-the-art pixel-level segmentation models fail in naively learning anatomical segmentation tasks due to domain memorization issues and conflicting labels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about failures of pixel-level models is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HybridGNet, a landmark-based segmentation model, learns available anatomical structures using graph-based representations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HybridGNet, a landmark-based segmentation model, learns available anatomical structures using graph-based representations. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution describing HybridGNet's design and objective is the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HybridGNet naturally learns more domain-invariant feature representations compared to traditional models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HybridGNet naturally learns more domain-invariant feature representations compared to traditional models. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim remains the same but the publication reports the result with reduced certainty, indicating a tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Empirical evidence supports the effectiveness of HybridGNet in the context of chest X-ray multiclass segmentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Empirical evidence supports the effectiveness of HybridGNet in the context of chest X-ray multiclass segmentation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Empirical support for HybridGNet on chest X-ray segmentation is identically reported in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study discovers temporal patterns in insulin need driven by both well-known and potentially novel factors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Further research is needed to identify and quantify the factors that cause unexpected insulin need patterns. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserts discovery of drivers including novel factors, while the publication reframes this as an open question requiring further work; the claim is therefore weakened/revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Various time series techniques, including matrix profile and multi-variate clustering, were utilized to identify these patterns. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The patterns in insulin needs were more pronounced when comparing hours of the day and similar days than when comparing days of the week or months. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a specific result produced by time-series analyses described in the preprint; the methods claim is thus clarified into a concrete pattern-finding with somewhat lower certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A better understanding of Type 1 Diabetes and the factors impacting insulin needs can contribute to the development of data-driven technology for T1D treatments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AID attempts to counteract both observed and unobserved factors that impact interstitial glucose levels. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements relate to the translational implication that automated/ data-driven systems must address observed and unobserved factors affecting glucose/insulin needs; the high-level implication is preserved though phrasing differs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fusion diagrams can be used to design new equivariant components for equivariant neural networks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed fusion diagrams lead to a diagrammatic approach for constructing novel neural network architectures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both statements describe the same contribution: using fusion diagrams to design new equivariant architectures.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting components, termed 'fusion blocks', serve as universal approximators of any continuous equivariant function defined in a local neighborhood. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fusion blocks serve as universal approximators of any continuous equivariant function defined on a local neighborhood. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The universality claim for fusion blocks is stated equivalently in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating a fusion block into pre-existing equivariant architectures leads to improved performance with fewer parameters on challenging chemical problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating a fusion block into pre-existing equivariant architectures improves performance with fewer parameters on challenging chemical problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance improvement claim is the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach combining tensor networks with equivariant neural networks suggests a potentially fruitful direction for designing more expressive equivariant neural networks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of tensor networks with equivariant neural networks suggests a potentially fruitful direction for designing more expressive equivariant neural networks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding suggestion about combining tensor networks and equivariant networks is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our transformer-based, neural-network architecture achieves an unprecedented authorship attribution accuracy, where up to 73% of papers are attributed correctly. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our transformer-based, neural-network architecture achieves an unprecedented authorship attribution accuracy, where up to 73% of papers are attributed correctly. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The wording and quantitative result are identical between preprint and publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We created the largest authorship identification dataset to date, leveraging over 2 million manuscripts from arXiv. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We created the largest authorship-identification dataset to date, leveraging over 2 million manuscripts from arXiv. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is the same in both versions with no substantive modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method provides empirical evidence of the key aspects that make a paper attributable. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We provide empirical evidence of the key aspects that make a paper attributable. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same claim; only minor rewording and a shift in label (result vs conclusion) occur, not changing the substantive content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present a scaling analysis that highlights the applicability of the proposed method to larger datasets. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method can be applied to even larger datasets when sufficient compute capabilities are available to the academic community. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.rationale: This claim appears verbatim with equivalent certainty; the publication preserves the same contribution statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our algorithm can significantly outperform baseline approaches in terms of coverage and bug finding.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implemented algorithm significantly outperforms baseline approaches in terms of coverage and bug finding on three large benchmarks.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same result but specifies evaluation context (three large benchmarks), narrowing the claim to documented benchmarks while otherwise maintaining confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper presents a general image formation model for albedo recovery from typical aerial photogrammetric images under natural illuminations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed image formation model successfully recovers albedo information from aerial photogrammetric images under natural illuminations.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same core contribution and outcome with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach outperforms existing methods for recovering albedo information in aerial photogrammetry.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This physics-based approach outperforms existing methods for albedo recovery in aerial photogrammetry.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same comparative performance claim with similar emphasis and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The recovered albedo image can improve typical image processing tasks in photogrammetry, such as feature and dense matching, edge, and line extraction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The recovered albedo image enhances typical image processing tasks in photogrammetry, including feature and dense matching, edge, and line extraction.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same downstream benefits with equivalent scope and medium certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The problem of recovering reflectance/albedo information from raw images remains unsolved in a practical context.
alignments.rationale: The publication omits this limitation/negative claim; no corresponding claim denying practical solvability appears in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fine-tuned BERT and Llama3 models achieved the highest performance in event extraction at 88.2-88.0 F1, comparable to the inter-annotator agreement of 88.4 F1.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fine-tuned BERT and Llama3 models achieved the highest performance in event extraction at 88.2-88.0 F1, comparable to the inter-annotator agreement of 88.4 F1.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this specific quantitative result verbatim, preserving scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In relation extraction, the fine-tuned BERT, Flan-T5, and Llama3 achieved the highest performance at 61.8-65.3 F1.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In relation extraction, the fine-tuned BERT, Flan-T5, and Llama3 achieved the highest performance at 61.8-65.3 F1.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this quantitative relation-extraction result with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fine-tuned models significantly outperformed GPT-4 in in-context learning, highlighting the importance of annotated training data and model optimization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fine-tuned models significantly outperformed GPT-4 in in-context learning, indicating the importance of annotated training data and model optimization.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the comparative finding and its interpretation with only minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For the task of information extraction, large language models (LLMs) offer no performance advantage over the smaller BERT models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Large language models offer no performance advantage over the smaller BERT models for the tasks evaluated.
alignments.rationale: The publication qualifies the generality by adding "for the tasks evaluated," narrowing the scope of the original broader claim while keeping the same conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multiple fine-tuned transformer models achieved performance comparable to inter-annotator agreement for several extraction tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fine-tuned BERT and Llama3 models achieved the highest performance in event extraction at 88.2-88.0 F1, comparable to the inter-annotator agreement of 88.4 F1.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a comparable-to-IAA result but only for event extraction rather than the broader "several extraction tasks" claim in the preprint, indicating a revision and narrowing of the original generalization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The public has a generally low level of understanding regarding fairness metrics in AI-driven healthcare recommendations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The public has a generally low level of understanding regarding fairness metrics in AI-driven healthcare recommendations.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this empirical result verbatim with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fairness is a complex and often misunderstood concept in the context of AI-driven decision-making systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fairness in AI-driven decision-making systems is a complex and often misunderstood concept.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conceptual conclusion with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A one-size-fits-all approach to fairness in AI systems may be insufficient.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A one-size-fits-all approach to fairness may be insufficient in developing equitable AI systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same cautionary conclusion, adding context about equitable AI systems but not changing the claim's thrust.

alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a need for enhanced information and education on algorithmic fairness to support informed decision-making.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduced deep learning architecture outputs 2D wall-shear stress fields with identical spatial resolution and domain size from wall-parallel velocity fields.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduced deep learning architecture can accurately output two-dimensional streamwise wall-shear stress fields from wall-parallel streamwise velocity fields.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes accuracy and streamwise components while omitting explicit mention of identical resolution/domain size; overall the core capability is preserved with clarified wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework acts as a surrogate model that encapsulates the mechanisms through which outer-layer flow structures influence wall-shear stress dynamics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework demonstrates zero-shot applicability to experimental velocity fields obtained from particle image velocimetry measurements.
alignments.rationale: The most similar published claim emphasizes zero-shot applicability (overlap with surrogate-model utility), and the publication increases the certainty/strength of the experimental applicability claim; matching confidence is moderate because the preprint framed this as a conceptual surrogate while the publication stresses demonstrated applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network demonstrates zero-shot applicability to experimental velocity fields obtained from Particle-Image Velocimetry measurements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework demonstrates zero-shot applicability to experimental velocity fields obtained from particle image velocimetry measurements.
alignments.rationale: This claim is preserved in the publication with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The physical accuracy of the wall-shear stress estimates is verified with synchronized wall-shear stress measurements for Reynolds numbers up to 2,000.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The wall-shear stress estimates produced by the framework are physically accurate when verified against synchronized wall-shear stress measurements using a micro-pillar shear-stress sensor.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the verification claim but specifies the sensor used (micro-pillar), clarifying the experimental validation details.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework facilitates advancements in various experimental applications by extracting inaccessible experimental wall-shear stress information from velocity measurements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework facilitates advancements in various experimental applications by extracting inaccessible experimental wall-shear stress information from readily available velocity measurements.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this concluding claim with equivalent meaning, adding wording that highlights the use of readily available measurements.

alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same observational claim that the diaphragm remained discernible after motion correction.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach lays the groundwork for the joint 3D quantification of fat fraction and water T1 in regions that are rarely studied, such as the respiratory muscles.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a higher-level contribution (groundwork for joint 3D quantification) that was not explicitly stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrating Artificial Intelligence into digital health applications can improve health system and public health performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating Artificial Intelligence into digital health applications can improve health system and public health performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level conclusion about AI improving health system and public health performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Artificial Intelligence and Reinforcement Learning platform allows the delivery of adaptive interventions that can be optimized through experimentation and real-time monitoring.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Causal Foundry Artificial Intelligence and Reinforcement Learning platform allows for the delivery of adaptive interventions that can be optimized through experimentation and real-time monitoring.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the platform's name (Causal Foundry) while preserving the original claim about adaptive, optimizable interventions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The platform's flexibility to connect to various mobile health applications and digital devices can significantly improve the impact of digital tools on health system outcomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The platform's flexibility to connect to various mobile health applications and digital devices can significantly improve the impact of digital tools on health system outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same claim about the platform's flexibility and its potential impact on health system outcomes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach discussed has the potential to be particularly impactful in resource-poor settings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach discussed has the potential to be particularly impactful in resource-poor settings.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the preprint's conclusion about potential impact in resource-poor settings without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed interactive framework enhances the explainability of face recognition models by combining model-agnostic Explainable Artificial Intelligence and Natural Language Processing techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed interactive framework enhances the explainability of face recognition models by combining model-agnostic Explainable Artificial Intelligence and Natural Language Processing techniques.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution about combining XAI and NLP to enhance explainability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The explanations generated by the proposed method are in the form of natural language text and visual representations that describe how different facial regions contribute to the similarity measure between two faces.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The explanations generated by the proposed method are in the form of natural language text and visual representations that describe how different facial regions contribute to the similarity measure between two faces.
alignments.rationale: The explanation format and content are preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach does not decrease the face recognition performance compared to previous studies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach does not decrease the face recognition performance compared to previous studies.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the performance-conservation claim with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework allows users to ask questions to obtain more precise information based on their background knowledge.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework allows users to ask questions to obtain more precise information based on their background knowledge.
alignments.rationale: The interactive, question-driven capability is carried forward without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method has the potential to make face recognition systems more interpretable and user-friendly, especially in sensitive applications where decision-making transparency is crucial.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method has the potential to make face recognition systems more interpretable and user-friendly, especially in sensitive applications where decision-making transparency is crucial.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same potential impact statement about interpretability and user-friendliness.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Continuous monitoring of PV modules during operation via unmanned aerial vehicles is essential to ensure that defective panels are promptly replaced or repaired.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Continuous monitoring of PV modules via images captured by unmanned aerial vehicles is essential to ensure prompt repair or replacement of defective panels.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same essential claim about the importance of UAV-based continuous monitoring for prompt maintenance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Computer vision provides an automatic, non-destructive and cost-effective tool for monitoring defects in large-scale PV plants.
alignments.rationale: The published paper does not explicitly restate this generalized claim about computer vision being non-destructive and cost-effective; it focuses instead on specific deep learning approaches and gaps.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most approaches use convolutional neural networks together with data augmentation or generative adversarial network-based techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most deep learning approaches for detecting defects in solar modules utilize convolutional neural networks with data augmentation or generative adversarial network-based techniques.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same observation about prevalent use of CNNs with augmentation or GAN-based techniques.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis reveals that the model focuses on the darker regions of the image to perform the classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluation of deep learning approaches reveals that the model focuses on the darker regions of the image to perform classification.
alignments.rationale: The evaluation finding about model attention to darker regions is preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review points towards a clear roadmap for making this technology commercially relevant.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are clear gaps in the existing approaches to defect detection in solar modules that need to be addressed.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's positive 'roadmap to commercial relevance' framing is changed in the publication to emphasize gaps and limitations, shifting tone and narrowing the claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrating weather data and geometric deep learning with existing approaches can enhance robustness and reliability in defect detection.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a forward-looking contribution recommending integration of weather data and geometric deep learning that did not appear in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The EAGLE model and the EAGLE-400K dataset provide a unified framework that integrates various egocentric video understanding tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The EAGLE model and the EAGLE-400K dataset provide a unified framework that integrates various egocentric video understanding tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution about EAGLE and EAGLE-400K providing a unified framework.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EAGLE-400K is the first large-scale instruction-tuning dataset tailored for egocentric video, featuring 400K diverse samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: EAGLE-400K is the first large-scale instruction-tuning dataset tailored for egocentric video, featuring 400K diverse samples.
alignments.rationale: The dataset claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EAGLE demonstrates superior performance over existing models in balancing task-specific understanding with holistic video interpretation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: EAGLE demonstrates superior performance over existing models in balancing task-specific understanding with holistic video interpretation.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim about EAGLE outperforming existing models is retained unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A set of evaluation metrics is proposed to facilitate a thorough assessment of MLLM for egocentric video understanding.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed evaluation metrics facilitate a thorough assessment of multimodal large language models for egocentric video understanding.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the abbreviation (MLLM) to 'multimodal large language models' while keeping the underlying contribution the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study performs a unified environmental state recognition for robots through spoken language using pre-trained large-scale vision-language models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study performs a unified environmental state recognition for robots through spoken language using pre-trained large-scale vision-language models.
alignments.rationale: Text and scope of the contribution are the same in preprint and publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method allows recognition of whether a room door is open/closed, a transparent door is open/closed, and whether water is running in a sink without training neural networks or manual programming.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method allows recognition of whether a room door is open/closed, a transparent door is open/closed, and whether water is running in a sink without training neural networks or manual programming.
alignments.rationale: The experimental recognition capabilities are described identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Recognition accuracy can be improved by selecting appropriate texts from a set of prepared texts based on black-box optimization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recognition accuracy can be improved by selecting appropriate texts from a set of prepared texts based on black-box optimization.
alignments.rationale: The optimization-based text selection claim appears verbatim with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method eliminates the need to prepare multiple different models and programs, facilitating the management of source code and computer resources.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach eliminates the need to prepare multiple different models and programs, facilitating the management of source code and computer resources.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about reducing model/program proliferation is preserved with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of the method is experimentally demonstrated through its application to the recognition behavior on a mobile robot, Fetch.
alignments.rationale: The publication omits the specific experimental demonstration on the Fetch robot present in the preprint, indicating removal of that claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Training noisy sequences and clean sequences with equal weights can negatively impact the performance of recommender systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Training noisy sequences and clean sequences with equal weights negatively impacts the performance of recommender models.
alignments.rationale: The core claim is the same, but the publication lowers stated certainty (high -> medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed self-supervised Auxiliary Task Joint Training (ATJT) method improves the reweighting of noisy sequences in recommender systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The self-supervised Auxiliary Task Joint Training (ATJT) method improves the performance of recommender systems by reweighting noisy sequences.
alignments.rationale: Both describe ATJT improving performance via reweighting noisy sequences; wording differs but substantive claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the self-supervised auxiliary task in enhancing the performance of the base model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results validate the effectiveness of the self-supervised auxiliary task in enhancing the base model’s performance.
alignments.rationale: Experimental validation claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method incorporates training results from a noise recognition model into the recommender model.
alignments.rationale: This procedural detail about incorporating noise recognition training results appears in the publication but is not present among the preprint claims, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduced deep learning framework enables effective dimensionality reduction and continuous representation of multi-altitude wind data from discrete observations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduced deep learning framework enables effective dimensionality reduction and continuous representation of multi-altitude wind data from discrete observations.
alignments.rationale: The principal contribution is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework improves data resolution across diverse climatic conditions to recover high-resolution details.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework improves data resolution across diverse climatic conditions to recover high-resolution details.
alignments.rationale: The super-resolution claim is preserved with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework reduces data dimensionality for more efficient storage of large climate datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach reduces data dimensionality for more efficient storage of large climate datasets.
alignments.rationale: Dimensionality reduction and storage efficiency claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach enables cross-prediction between wind data measured at different heights.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework enables cross-prediction between wind data measured at different heights.
alignments.rationale: The claim is retained but the publication reports a lower certainty (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Comprehensive testing confirms that the approach surpasses existing methods in both super-resolution quality and compression efficiency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Comprehensive testing confirms that the approach surpasses existing methods in both super-resolution quality and compression efficiency.
alignments.rationale: Benchmarking/comparative performance claim remains the same and is kept with high certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simulation methodology described can augment and reduce the reliance on live experiments for evaluating policies in recommender systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The simulation methodology described can augment and reduce the reliance on live experiments for evaluating policies in recommender systems.
alignments.rationale: The contribution about reducing dependence on live experiments is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Counterfactually robust user behavior models enable reliable predictions of new algorithms' performance on key metrics when deployed live.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Counterfactually robust user behavior models enable reliable predictions of algorithm performance on key metrics when deployed live.
alignments.rationale: The claim about counterfactually robust models predicting live performance is retained with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deployment of the simulation service effectively couples user behavior models with production infrastructure for testing algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deployment of the simulation methodology for preference elicitation algorithms on the YouTube Music platform demonstrates its effectiveness in evaluating onboarding processes.
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the deployment claim to a specific platform and task (YouTube Music onboarding) and reports it as a demonstrated result with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The costs associated with A/B testing can be particularly problematic during the onboarding of new users, as this process occurs only once.
alignments.rationale: The discussion of A/B testing costs for onboarding users is not present in the publication's claims and appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The HazeSpace2M dataset, a collection of over 2 million images, enhances dehazing through haze type classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The HazeSpace2M dataset, which includes over 2 million images with diverse haze types, enhances dehazing through haze type classification.
alignments.rationale: The dataset contribution is present in both versions with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Haze type classification followed by specialized dehazing improves results by 2.41% in PSNR, 17.14% in SSIM, and 10.2% in MSE over general dehazers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using haze type classification followed by specialized dehazing improves results by 2.41% in PSNR, 17.14% in SSIM, and 10.2% in MSE over general dehazers.
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance improvements are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Benchmarking with state-of-the-art models shows that ResNet50 and AlexNet achieve 92.75% and 92.50% accuracy, respectively, against existing synthetic datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ResNet50 and AlexNet achieve 92.75% and 92.50% accuracy, respectively, against existing synthetic datasets, but only 80% and 70% accuracy against the Real Hazy Testset (RHT).
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the synthetic-dataset accuracies and adds real-testset results, providing additional context and broadening the empirical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Applying the proposed framework significantly improves the performance of state-of-the-art dehazing models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Applying the proposed framework significantly improves the performance of state-of-the-art dehazing models.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about improved SOTA model performance is preserved with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The challenging nature of the HazeSpace2M dataset is highlighted by the lower accuracy of SOTA models against the Real Hazy Testset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ResNet50 and AlexNet achieve 92.75% and 92.50% accuracy, respectively, against existing synthetic datasets, but only 80% and 70% accuracy against the Real Hazy Testset (RHT).
alignments.rationale: The publication's added real-testset accuracies directly support the preprint's claim that the dataset is challenging; the substantive point is preserved.
